title: Skills System
description: The 18 skills that represent categories of activities for ability checks

overview.description: Most ability checks involve using a skill, which represents a category of things creatures try to do with an ability check
overview.rule_references: The descriptions of the actions you take specify which skill applies if you make an ability check for that action, and many other rules note when a skill is relevant
overview.dm_authority: The DM has the ultimate say on whether a skill is relevant in a situation

skills.name: Acrobatics
example_uses: Stay on your feet in a tricky situation

skills.name: Animal Handling
example_uses: Get an animal to behave in a certain way

skills.name: Arcana
example_uses: Recall lore about the planes of existence

skills.name: History
example_uses: Recall lore about people, nations, and cultures

skills.name: Insight
example_uses: Discern a person's mood and intentions

skills.name: Intimidation
example_uses: Awe or threaten someone into doing what you want

skills.name: Medicine
example_uses: Determine what killed the recently slain

skills.name: Nature
example_uses: Recall lore about plants, animals, and weather

skills.name: Perception
example_uses: Using a combination of senses, notice something that's easy to miss

skills.name: Persuasion
example_uses: Honestly and graciously convince someone of something

social.focus: Interacting with and influencing other creatures

knowledge.focus: Recalling information and applying learned knowledge

practical.focus: Applied skills for survival and health

variant_abilities.description: Each skill proficiency is associated with an ability check. In some situations, the DM might allow you to apply your skill proficiency to a different ability check.
variant_abilities.example: If a character tries to intimidate someone through a show of physical strength, the DM might ask for a Strength (Intimidation) check rather than a Charisma (Intimidation) check. That character would make a Strength check and add their Proficiency Bonus if they have Intimidation proficiency.

common_variants.scenario: Physical intimidation through shows of strength
Endurance-based athletic activities like marathon running
Gymnastic feats requiring upper body strength

proficiency_mechanics.with_proficiency: Add Proficiency Bonus to ability checks involving that skill
proficiency_mechanics.without_proficiency: Can still make ability checks but don't add Proficiency Bonus
proficiency_mechanics.tool_synergy: If you have proficiency in both a relevant skill and tool, you have Advantage on the check
